content:
1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, the refactor agent, or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	- archon-lean-lsp: Lean LSP MCP server (project scope) — use for all Lean LSP operations (search, diagnostics, goal inspection)
19	- archon-informal-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` (symlinked from Archon) — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini) for informal mathematical reasoning
20	
21	## Key Files & Permissions
22	
23	All state files are in `.archon/`:
24	
25	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Refactor Agent | Review Agent | User |
26	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
27	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | read only | read only | read |
28	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
29	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
30	| `.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` | **write** | do not read | **read** (then execute) | do not read | read |
31	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | read only | read only | read |
32	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | read only | read only | read |
33	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (refactor.md) | read only | read |
34	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
35	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
36	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read + limited write** (file path rename only) | read | **write** |
37	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | **write (all files; protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed)** | do not edit | write (via comments) |
38	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
39	
40	## Protected Declarations
41	
42	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
43	
44	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
45	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
46	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed. 
47	
48	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
49	
50	The blueprint uses two active markers.
51	
52	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
53	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
54	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
55	
56	**The review agent is the sole writer of these markers.** Prover agents (autoformalize / prover / polish) never touch the blueprint chapters — they describe their outcome in `task_results/<file>.md` and the review agent verifies and marks accordingly. The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints, but never markers.
57	
58	## User Interaction
59	
60	Users provide hints in two places:
61	
62	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
63	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
64	
65	## Agent Roles
66	
67	### Plan Agent
68	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
69	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
70	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
71	- Read `.archon/task_results/refactor.md` - if refactor agent has run, read their report and adjust your plans accordingly
72	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
73	- Write `.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` when structural changes are needed
74	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
75	
76	### Prover Agent
77	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
78	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
79	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
80	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
81	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
82	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
83	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
84	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
85	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
86	
87	### Refactor Agent
88	- Read `.archon/prompts/refactor.md` for your full instructions
89	- Read `.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` for the plan agent's directive
90	- Modify any `.lean` file: definitions, signatures, types, imports
91	- Keep all files compiling (insert `sorry` at broken proof sites)
92	- Do NOT fill proofs — that's the prover's job
93	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/refactor.md`
94	
95	### Review Agent
96	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
97	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
98	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
99	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
100	- **Update blueprint markers** (`\leanok`, `\mathlibok`) in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` based on verified compilation and the provers' task results
101	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md

init.md
lane-merge.md
plan.md
prover-autoformalize.md
prover-polish.md
prover-prover.md
refactor-draft.md
refactor.md
review.md

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You are the prover agent in the proving stage. Your job: fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for context on your assigned file — prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas
9	3. Check your `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments — these are file-specific hints from the user
10	4. Before writing Lean code, you **MUST** consult the relevant blueprint chapter. Blueprints contain mathematical proof sketches; your formal proof must align with them. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs, pushing as far as possible
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you cannot fully prove a sorry, replace it with your best attempt — commented-out proof steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with remaining `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` for the stuck parts), but your work must be visible in the code for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to the original bare `sorry` — that wastes all your work.
13	7. Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md` — what you tried, what worked, what's stuck, next steps
14	
15	**Write permissions**: You may only write to your assigned `.lean` file(s) and your `task_results/<file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	Before editing, read `archon-protected.yaml` at the project root. Declarations listed for your file have **frozen signatures** — you may fill their proof bodies but must not rename them, change their types, reorder their arguments, or weaken their hypotheses. Only the mathematician is allowed to edit protected signatures. 
20	
21	## Avoid Early Termination
22	
23	- Do not abandon a proof prematurely
24	- Do not delegate to the next iteration or another prover if it can be solved with more effort
25	- Many complex problems require thousands of lines of Lean code
26	- Do not stop and leave a sorry simply because the proof is long
27	- Task difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave `sorry` placeholders
28	- Only modify the proof corresponding to the task; leave other proofs/declarations untouched
29	- **Decomposition**: Act like a mathematician — systematically break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure if available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each one individually until the entire goal is closed
30	
31	## Task Completion Criteria
32	
33	Your task is NOT complete until ALL of:
34	1. Every `sorry` has been replaced with a complete proof
35	2. Zero axioms introduced
36	3. The file compiles successfully with no errors
37	
38	If you encounter obstacles:
39	- Break the problem into smaller subgoals
40	- Search for relevant Mathlib lemmas more thoroughly
41	- Prove missing helper lemmas yourself
42	- Try alternative proof strategies
43	- Consult the informal proof / blueprint for guidance
44	- Use Web Search to find paper proofs when Mathlib lacks a theorem
45	
46	### When infrastructure is missing or the current route is too hard
47	
48	Do NOT just report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up on a sorry, you must try to find an alternative yourself:
49	
50	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`) — ask: "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only using tools available in Lean 4 Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
51	2. **Try the alternative** — if the informal agent gives you a route, attempt to formalize it.
52	3. **If you still can't solve it**, save what you learned for the plan agent:
53	   - Write the informal agent's alternative proof sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md`
54	   - In your `task_results/<file>.md`, record: what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found (even if unverified). This gives the plan agent concrete material to work with — not just "it's hard."
55	   - A prover that reports "I couldn't prove X, but here's an alternative approach via Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than one that just says "infrastructure missing."
56	
57	## Proof Style
58	
59	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its proof body
60	- Keep edits minimal: do not delete comments or change labels
61	- Do not add unrelated declarations
62	- **Intermediate helper lemmas you introduced** may be modified if they turn out to be incorrect or need adjustment.
63	- Add concise, informative comments above helper lemmas to make later reuse easy
64	
65	## Search Protocol
66	
67	Follow the search tool priority and query guidance in the lean4 skill reference (`references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`). Key points:
68	
69	1. `lean_local_search` first
70	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — **describe the mathematical content**, not just the name
71	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only
72	4. Never use local file search (find, grep) to locate Mathlib theorems
73	
74	## Missing Lemmas & Impossibility
75	
76	Follow the lean4 skill reference (`references/sorry-filling.md`) for:
77	- **When Mathlib lacks a theorem**: bypass or implement yourself. Web Search for published papers. Never leave a `sorry` just because Mathlib doesn't have it.
78	- **Distinguish impossibility from difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
79	
80	## Logging
81	
82	Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Use the file name from your assigned `.lean` file (e.g., if you own `Algebra/WLocal.lean`, write to `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`).
83	
84	**Format:**
85	
86	```markdown
87	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
88	
89	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
90	### Attempt 1
91	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
92	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance not available
93	- **Dead end:** Do not try direct case split without IsLocalRing
94	
95	### Attempt 2
96	- **Approach:** Use Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
97	- **Result:** RESOLVED
98	- **Key insight:** Mathlib's PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective bridges the gap
99	
100	## helper_bijective (line 78)
101	### Attempt 1
102	- **Approach:** Function.Bijective.comp
103	- **Result:** IN PROGRESS — stuck on surjectivity
104	- **Next step:** Try PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective
105	- **Relevant lemmas found:** PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective
106	```
107	
108	**Rules:**
109	1. One section per theorem/lemma in your file
110	2. Each attempt records: approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps
111	3. Log negative search results (e.g., "Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib")
112	4. The plan agent collects these files and merges them into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`
113	
114	**Read-only references:** Read `task_pending.md` for prior context on your file. Read `task_done.md` if the current problem resembles a completed one. Do not write to either.
115	
116	## Summary Pipeline
117	
118	1. Read `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md` for context from prior sessions
119	2. Read the informal proof / blueprint to understand the proof strategy and lemma decomposition
120	3. Introduce helper lemmas (matching the blueprint's structure) in the `.lean` file
121	4. Replace `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs, ensuring the file compiles without errors
122	5. Do not modify protected declarations listed in `archon-protected.yaml`.
123	6. Use Mathlib theorems when possible. Use Web Search when Mathlib lacks referenced results
124	7. Rely on Lean LSP for diagnostics; use `lake env lean <file>` sparingly for final checks
125	8. Log all explorations in `task_results/<your_file>.md`
126	
127	## End-of-session handoff
128	
129	Before you stop (or when you are done with this round of work):
130	
131	1. Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` with:
132	   - Current result (IN PROGRESS / FAILED) and what you tried
133	   - Any Mathlib lemmas you discovered that are relevant
134	   - Concrete next step for the next session
135	   - Dead-end warnings for approaches that won't work
136	2. Save all file changes (ensure compilation passes, using scoped `sorry` if needed)
137

1	# Project Progress
2	
3	## Current Stage
4	prover  (iter-005 = first prover round on the iter-004 helper scaffolds)
5	
6	## Stages
7	- [x] init
8	- [x] autoformalize
9	- [ ] prover  (iter-005 prover round on the two iter-004 helper scaffolds)
10	- [ ] polish
11	
12	## Big-picture context
13	
14	This project formalizes the Jacobian of a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over a field, following Christian Merten's challenge (`references/challenge.lean`). The nine declarations in `archon-protected.yaml` are the deliverables — their signatures are frozen.
15	
16	`STRATEGY.md` describes the multi-phase build-out path (Phases A–E). All 9 protected sorries remain blocked behind missing Mathlib infrastructure. Iter-004 (refactor) scaffolded two new helper files in two parallel low-coupling tracks, raising the sorry count from 9 → 12 (the +3 are the new scaffold sites). Iter-005 (this prover round) closes two of those three new sorries; the third (`PicardFunctor.representable`) is intentionally deferred.
17	
18	## Iteration 005 — first prover round on the iter-004 helper scaffolds
19	
20	The refactor agent's iter-004 report (`task_results/refactor.md`) is verified:
21	
22	- New files compile cleanly (`lean_diagnostic_messages` returns only the expected sorry warnings).
23	- Sorry counts: `Cohomology/SheafCompose.lean` = 1, `Picard/Functor.lean` = 2, all other files unchanged. Project total = 12 (9 protected + 3 new). Matches the directive's expected post-refactor count exactly.
24	- Protected signatures: untouched. `archon-protected.yaml` byte-identical.
25	- No new axioms introduced (`Grep '^axiom' AlgebraicJacobian/` returns nothing).
26	- Blueprint `\lean{...}` macros: already correct in `Cohomology_SheafCompose.tex` and `Picard_Functor.tex` — no chapter edits needed.
27	
28	Two minor refactor-time deviations from the directive's sketch are documented in `task_results/refactor.md` and require no follow-up action:
29	
30	1. `Opens.grothendieckTopology` (not `TopologicalSpace.Opens.grothendieckTopology`) — Mathlib idiom.
31	2. `PicardFunctor` takes `(C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))` rather than `(C : Scheme.{u}) [C.Over ...]` — the bundled-over shape avoids a typeclass-inference dead-end for `?k` and matches the existing convention in `Jacobian.lean`.
32	
33	## Current Objectives
34	
35	The objectives are split between two prover agents (one per file). The third existing protected file group (`Genus.lean` / `Jacobian.lean` / `AbelJacobi.lean`) is **off-limits** this iteration: its 9 sorries remain blocked on upstream Phase A / C / E infrastructure that this iteration does not deliver.
36	
37	### 1. **`AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/SheafCompose.lean`** — fill `instHasSheafCompose_forget_CommRing_AddCommGrp`
38	
39	- **Sorry**: 1 (the single instance, line 41 in the new file).
40	- **Blueprint**: `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_SheafCompose.tex` (theorem `thm:HasSheafCompose_forget`).
41	- **Final declaration name**: `AlgebraicGeometry.Cohomology.instHasSheafCompose_forget_CommRing_AddCommGrp`.
42	- **Why this is the priority**: smallest unblocking unit toward `genus`. Provable from current Mathlib (`b80f227`) using algebraic-forgetful-functor limit-preservation.
43	
44	**Strategy (taken from the blueprint chapter; repeated here so the prover does not have to retrieve it):**
45	
46	1. Use `lean_local_search` / `lean_leansearch` to identify the most direct existing Mathlib lemma that produces `HasSheafCompose` from limit preservation by the post-composing functor — e.g. `Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_comp` or `HasSheafCompose.isSheaf_of_preservesMultiequalizer`.
47	2. Establish that `forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⋙ forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat` preserves the relevant limits (multiequalizers indexed by sieve covers in the topology of opens). In Mathlib, both `forget₂` instances are `HasForget₂` morphisms of structure-forgetful functors; they preserve all small limits.
48	3. Combine to produce the instance and close the sorry. Body should be at most ~20 lines.
49	
50	**Mathlib API to lean on (search with `lean_leansearch` / `lean_loogle`):**
51	
52	- `CategoryTheory.Presheaf.IsSheaf` and `Presheaf.IsSheaf.isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_comp`.
53	- `CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.HasSheafCompose` typeclass and its constructors.
54	- `CategoryTheory.HasForget₂` for `CommRingCat → RingCat` and `RingCat → AddCommGrpCat`; the resulting `forget₂` functors preserve limits.
55	- `CategoryTheory.preservesMultiequalizer_of_preservesLimitsOfShape` or analogous.
56	- The general `HasSheafCompose` factory is sometimes called `GrothendieckTopology.HasSheafCompose.mk` / `…of_preservesLimit` in Mathlib — check both.
57	
58	**Reject conditions** (the plan agent will reject the result if):
59	
60	- The instance is closed by `decide`, `trivial`, or any tactic that leaves the body morally vacuous.
61	- A new `axiom` is introduced.
62	- An ad-hoc weakened hypothesis is added (e.g. restricting `X` to be sober / Hausdorff / etc.).
63	- The proof inserts an `unfold`/`change` to a term that is not the stated `HasSheafCompose` instance.
64	
65	### 2. **`AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/Functor.lean`** — fill `PicardFunctor` (definition only)
66	
67	- **Sorries**: 2 in the file. **Fill the first one only** (`PicardFunctor`, line 65). The second (`PicardFunctor.representable`, line 81) is intentionally deferred — see "Forbidden" below.
68	- **Blueprint**: `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_Functor.tex` (definition `def:Pic_functor`; theorem `thm:Pic_representable` is the deferred sorry).
69	- **Final declaration names**:
70	  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicardFunctor` — to be filled (the definition).
71	  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicardFunctor.representable` — to be **left as `sorry`**.
72	- **Why**: smallest constructible Phase C step on top of iter-003's `LineBundle` / `Pic.pullback` API. The functor itself is a concrete categorical construction; only its representability is hard.
73	
74	**Strategy (taken from the blueprint chapter):**
75	
76	1. For each `S : Schemeᵒᵖ`, define
77	   `(PicardFunctor C).obj S := QuotientGroup.mk' (Pic.pullback (p_S : (C ×_k S.unop) ⟶ S.unop)).range`,
78	   i.e. the cokernel-as-quotient-group of the pullback homomorphism `Pic(S) → Pic(C ×_k S)`. The `Pic.pullback` lemmas `pullback_id` and `pullback_comp` (closed in iter-003) are needed for functoriality.
79	2. For each morphism `f : S' ⟶ S` in `Schemeᵒᵖ` (i.e. `f.unop : S.unop ⟶ S'.unop`), the induced map on `C ×_k S → C ×_k S'` is `pullback.map _ _ _ _ (𝟙 C) f.unop _` (or analogous fiber-product naturality), and the induced quotient map is `QuotientGroup.lift _ ((Pic.pullback (pullback.map …)).comp QuotientGroup.mk') _ _` — straightforward, just careful bookkeeping of the universal properties.
80	3. Functoriality (`map_id`, `map_comp`) is automatic from `Functor.map_id` / `Functor.map_comp` in most cases; if not, decompose into explicit `simp` lemmas of length ≤ 5 lines each.
81	4. Note the binder shape used in the file: `(C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))` (bundled over-category object). The structure morphism is therefore `C.hom : C.left ⟶ Spec (CommRingCat.of k)`, and the underlying scheme is `C.left`. Inside the body, the fiber product `C.left ×_k S.unop` is a `Limits.pullback` of `C.hom` against `S.unop ⟶ Spec (CommRingCat.of k)` (assumed via the implicit `S` having a structure morphism to `Spec k`; if it does not, the directive's blueprint asks for the over-category construction `Sch / Spec k`, but the iter-005 prover may take `S : Schemeᵒᵖ` and build the fiber product against `C.hom` from the available scheme structure). Document the chosen shape in the task result.
82	
83	**Forbidden** (the plan agent will reject the result if):
84	
85	- The functor is closed by `Discrete PUnit`, `Discrete Unit`, or any other vacuous functor.
86	- The `representable` sorry is *also* filled — even with a "trivial" closure that exploits the global-sections-approximate `LineBundle`. The forward-compatibility note in the file docstring and in the blueprint chapter is normative: closing `representable` here would silently assert the wrong theorem and is a forbidden shortcut. **Leave the `representable` sorry in place.**
87	- A new `axiom` is introduced.
88	- The signature is weakened (e.g. dropping a typeclass).
89	
90	**Mathlib API to lean on (search with `lean_leansearch` / `lean_loogle`):**
91	
92	- `CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback`, `pullback.fst`, `pullback.snd`, `pullback.map` (fiber product of schemes; the binary one suffices for `C ×_k S`).
93	- `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.LineBundle` (this project, iter-003 — for `Pic`).
94	- `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Pic.pullback` (this project, iter-003 — for the quotient subgroup).
95	- `QuotientGroup.mk'`, `QuotientGroup.lift`, `QuotientGroup.con` for the quotient and its universal property.
96	- `MonoidHom.range`, `Subgroup.normal_of_comm` (or `CommGroup`'s automatic normality of all subgroups) for the quotient subgroup.
97	
98	If a constituent lemma is genuinely missing in Mathlib, document the gap in `task_results/Picard_Functor.lean.md` with the exact missing declaration name and intended type — do **not** fill the sorry with an ad-hoc placeholder.
99	
100	### 3. The 3 protected files (`Genus.lean`, `Jacobian.lean`, `AbelJacobi.lean`) — DO NOT TOUCH
101	
102	Their 9 sorries remain blocked on upstream infrastructure (Phase A: coherent sheaf cohomology; Phase C: Picard scheme & representability; Phase E: image generation + dual abelian variety). The dispatcher should not assign provers to them in iter-005. If a prover is somehow dispatched against any of these files, it must:
103	
104	1. **Stop** and write a one-line `task_results/<file>.md` saying "no objective for protected files this iteration; see PROGRESS.md."
105	2. **Not** modify any `.lean` file.
106	
107	## Anticipated iter-006+
108	
109	- **If iter-005 closes Cohomology/SheafCompose (objective 1):** the next refactor will scaffold Phase A step 2 (`HasSheafify (Opens.gT X) AddCommGrpCat`). Plan agent will check whether Mathlib's `HasSheafify` infrastructure (currently for set-valued Grothendieck-topology sheaves; check `CategoryTheory.Sheafify` for `AddCommGrpCat` valued sheaves) is directly applicable; if not, a longer step-2 scaffolding plan will be needed.
110	- **If iter-005 closes Picard/Functor objective 2 (definition):** the next refactor scaffolds Phase C step 3 (étale sheafification of the Picard presheaf). The étale Grothendieck topology on `Sch_S` may need to be set up first — Mathlib's `EllAdicCohomology` carries some étale machinery but it is not the relative-Picard input we need.
111	- **If iter-005 partially fails on Cohomology (e.g. limit-preservation lemma is not in the form needed):** plan agent will (a) consult the informal agent for an alternative route, (b) decompose the proof obligation into smaller helper instances, (c) re-issue.
112	- **If iter-005 partially fails on Picard/Functor (e.g. `Limits.pullback` of schemes is not the standard route):** plan agent will (a) check `AlgebraicGeometry.Pullback` API in Mathlib for the scheme-specific construction, (b) re-issue with the corrected API names.
113	
114	## Output protocol
115	
116	Each prover writes to `.archon/task_results/<file>.md`. Required content:
117	
118	- list of sorries closed (file:line, declaration name)
119	- list of new helper lemmas added (declaration name + one-line statement)
120	- list of remaining sorries with reason (e.g. "still blocked on missing Mathlib lemma `Foo.bar`" with exact name + intended type)
121	- compile status (`lake build` exit, sorry count after)
122	- which `\leanok` markers the review agent should add (the prover does *not* edit blueprint chapters)
123	
124	The plan agent will independently verify (sorry count + `lean_diagnostic_messages` + axiom check) before declaring success.
125	
126	## Known dead ends (do not retry)
127	
128	- Forbidden-shortcut definitions documented in `task_pending.md` (`LineBundle X := Unit`, `genus C := 0`, `Jacobian C := 𝟙_ _`, `ofCurve P := constant`, `PicardFunctor C := Discrete PUnit`) are mathematically wrong and rejected on sight.
129	- Closing `PicardFunctor.representable` sorry on top of the global-sections-approximate `LineBundle` would assert representability of the wrong functor and is a forbidden shortcut. Keep that sorry in place in iter-005.
130	- Re-running discovery searches on the 9 protected sorries is unproductive: the Phase A / C / E gaps are verified and stable until upstream Mathlib changes.
131	- The point-wise topological-equality form of rigidity (`∀ x ∈ U, g₁.left.base x = g₂.left.base x ⇒ g₁ = g₂`) is mathematically false in characteristic `p` (Frobenius counterexample). Use the scheme-level form as in iter-003.
132	- `MonoidalCategory X.Modules` is absent in Mathlib `b80f227` (verified iter-003); building it is a multi-iteration project and is not in scope for iter-005.
133	
134	## Related state files
135	
136	- `STRATEGY.md` — five-phase build-out plan; revision log records iter-001 / iter-002 / iter-003 / iter-004 changes. No revision in iter-005 (no strategic change anticipated this round).
137	- `task_pending.md` — consolidated gap report and helper-target index. Updated to mark Track B step 1 and Track C step 2 as "ASSIGNED iter-005 prover".
138	- `task_done.md` — iter-003 closures: `eq_of_eqOnOpen`, `LineBundle`, `instCommGroupLineBundle`, `Pic.pullback`. Will gain iter-005 entries on successful completion.
139	- `REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` — cleared (iter-004 directive was executed; no new directive is written this pass).
140	- `PROJECT_STATUS.md` — written by the review agent after iter-003 (session_2). Will be updated by the review agent after iter-005.
141	- `proof-journal/sessions/session_1/` — iter-001 / iter-002 review.
142	- `proof-journal/sessions/session_2/` — iter-003 review with full recommendations for iter-004.
143

The PROGRESS.md explicitly states `Genus.lean` is OFF-LIMITS for iter-005 (it is one of the 3 protected files where the 9 sorries remain blocked on upstream Phase A/C/E infrastructure). The dispatcher instruction is unambiguous: stop, write a one-line task result, do not modify any `.lean` file.

Let me verify the file exists and confirm before writing the result.

-rw-rw-r-- 1 archon archon 4225 May  6 11:27 /home/archon/Lean_tests/AlgebraicJacobian/AlgebraicJacobian/Genus.lean

/home/archon/Lean_tests/AlgebraicJacobian/.archon/task_results/:
total 8
drwxrwxr-x 2 archon archon 4096 May  6 14:24 .
drwxrwxr-x 7 archon archon 4096 May  6 14:24 ..

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	import Mathlib
7	
8	/-!
9	# Genus of a smooth proper curve
10	
11	The genus of a smooth, proper, geometrically irreducible curve over a field.
12	
13	## Status (iteration 001 — discovery)
14	
15	Mathlib b80f227 has the abstract Grothendieck-topology sheaf-cohomology API
16	(`CategoryTheory.Sheaf.H`) but no specialisation that yields the
17	$k$-vector-space dimension of $H^1(C, \mathcal O_C)$ for $C$ a smooth proper
18	curve over $k$. See `task_results/Genus.md` for the precise gap. The `sorry`
19	below is left in place per the discovery directive in `.archon/PROGRESS.md`.
20	
21	### Building blocks already in Mathlib
22	
23	* `CategoryTheory.Sheaf.H : Sheaf J AddCommGrpCat → ℕ → Type w'` — abstract
24	  cohomology with `AddCommGroup` instance (`Sheaf.instAddCommGroupH`),
25	  living in `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/Basic.lean`.
26	* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.sheaf : TopCat.Sheaf CommRingCat X` — the
27	  structure sheaf in commutative-ring form.
28	* `TopCat.Sheaf C X` is *definitionally* `Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) C`.
29	* `RingCat.hasForgetToAddCommGrp : HasForget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat`.
30	* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules X` and `SheafOfModules.toSheaf` for the
31	  $\mathcal O_X$-module category and its forget to `Sheaf J AddCommGrpCat`.
32	* `IsGrothendieckAbelian.hasExt` : provides `HasExt` on Grothendieck-abelian
33	  sheaf categories.
34	
35	### Missing pieces (Phase A of `STRATEGY.md`)
36	
37	1. A `Sheaf (Opens.gT C.toPresheafedSpace.carrier) AddCommGrpCat` built from
38	   `C.left.sheaf` via `forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⋙ forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat`,
39	   with `HasSheafCompose` instance (sheaf condition preserved by the forgetful).
40	2. The instance `HasSheafify (Opens.gT X) AddCommGrpCat`.
41	3. The instance `HasExt (Sheaf (Opens.gT X) AddCommGrpCat)`.
42	4. A `Module k`-structure on the resulting `H 1` group, derived from the
43	   $k$-algebra structure on $\Gamma(C, \mathcal O_C)$ induced by `C.hom`.
44	5. (For meaningful `finrank`) finite-dimensionality, i.e. Serre's theorem on
45	   coherent cohomology of a proper $k$-scheme. `Module.finrank` returns `0`
46	   without it, so step 4 alone makes the definition type-check; step 5 is
47	   needed for downstream theorems.
48	
49	The sketch below documents what an honest definition would look like once
50	those pieces exist; it is intentionally commented out so the file compiles.
51	-/
52	
53	set_option autoImplicit false
54	
55	universe u
56	
57	open CategoryTheory Limits
58	
59	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
60	
61	/-
62	-- Sketch of the route once Phase A is available:
63	--
64	-- noncomputable def OXAsAddCommGrpSheaf
65	--     {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k))) :
66	--     CategoryTheory.Sheaf
67	--       (TopologicalSpace.Opens.grothendieckTopology
68	--         C.left.toPresheafedSpace.carrier) AddCommGrpCat :=
69	--   ⟨C.left.sheaf.1.comp
70	--     (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⋙ forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat), by
71	--     -- Use `Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_comp` plus the fact that
72	--     -- forgetful functors `CommRingCat ⥤ RingCat ⥤ AddCommGrpCat`
73	--     -- create limits of multispan diagrams.
74	--     sorry⟩
75	--
76	-- noncomputable def H1OC
77	--     {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k))) : Type _ :=
78	--   (OXAsAddCommGrpSheaf C).H 1
79	--
80	-- The `Module k`-instance on `H1OC` would come from the `k`-algebra
81	-- structure on `Γ(C, O_C)` (via `C.hom : C.left ⟶ Spec (.of k)`),
82	-- propagated through the abelian-group cohomology by functoriality.
83	-/
84	
85	-- data
86	/-- The genus of a smooth proper curve. -/
87	def genus {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
88	    [IsProper C.hom] [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
89	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] : ℕ :=
90	  -- Honest definition is `Module.finrank k (H¹(C, O_C))`, where the latter is
91	  -- a `k`-vector space via `C.hom : C.left ⟶ Spec (.of k)`. The current Mathlib
92	  -- API (b80f227) does not assemble these pieces; see the docstring above and
93	  -- `.archon/task_results/Genus.md` for the precise gap and helper-lemma
94	  -- proposals. Discovery iteration 001 leaves the sorry intentionally per
95	  -- `.archon/PROGRESS.md` (no constant placeholder, no axiom).
96	  sorry
97	
98	end AlgebraicGeometry
99
Confirmed — `Genus.lean` is on the explicit DO-NOT-TOUCH list for iter-005. Writing the stop-out task result and making no `.lean` edits.

# AlgebraicJacobian/Genus.lean

## Status: STOPPED — no objective this iteration

No objective for protected files this iteration; see PROGRESS.md.

## Detail

`PROGRESS.md` (iter-005, "Current Objectives", §3) explicitly designates the
three protected files (`Genus.lean`, `Jacobian.lean`, `AbelJacobi.lean`) as
**DO NOT TOUCH** for this prover round. Their sorries remain blocked on
upstream Phase A / C / E infrastructure (coherent sheaf cohomology of a proper
$k$-scheme, Picard-scheme representability, image generation + dual abelian
variety) that is not delivered in iter-005.

Per the dispatcher's emergency instruction in `PROGRESS.md`:

> If a prover is somehow dispatched against any of these files, it must:
> 1. **Stop** and write a one-line `task_results/<file>.md` saying
>    "no objective for protected files this iteration; see PROGRESS.md."
> 2. **Not** modify any `.lean` file.

Both clauses are honored:

- No edit was made to `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus.lean` (or any other `.lean`
  file). The single existing `sorry` in `genus` (line 96) is unchanged. The
  file's own docstring already records the precise Phase-A gap (steps 1–5)
  needed to give `genus` an honest definition; nothing in iter-005 narrows that
  gap.
- The two iter-005 prover objectives are dispatched to the iter-004 helper
  scaffolds (`Cohomology/SheafCompose.lean` and `Picard/Functor.lean`), not to
  the protected files. The eventual unblock for `genus` will require the
  Phase-A successor of objective 1 (`HasSheafCompose_forget` → `HasSheafify` →
  `HasExt` → `Module k` instance on `H¹(C, O_C)` → finite-dimensionality), as
  outlined in `PROGRESS.md` "Anticipated iter-006+".

## Sorries closed

None.

## Helpers added

None.

## Remaining sorries in this file

1 — `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus.lean:96`, `AlgebraicGeometry.genus`. Status:
**unchanged**, blocked on Phase A (see file docstring lines 35–47).

## Compile status

Not re-checked by this prover (no edit performed). The plan agent's iter-004
verification (`PROGRESS.md` "Iteration 005" §1) confirms the project compiles
cleanly with exactly the 12 expected sorry warnings, of which 1 lives in this
file.

## Blueprint markers

No marker change requested. The genus chapter
(`blueprint/src/chapters/AlgebraicJacobian_Genus.tex`) should remain in its
current marker state (no `\leanok` on the `genus` definition: it is formalized
only as a `sorry`-bodied stub, and per the project's marker vocabulary,
`\leanok` on a *statement* requires at least a typed declaration — which is
present — but the iter-005 review pass should re-confirm whether the existing
marker state already reflects this; no change is made by this prover).
